message:
type: PROPOSE
my_files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py
note: I need to add normalize_image_bytes helper and refactor image_to_base64 in types.py. If your feature also touches this file, let's coordinate on non-overlapping regions.

type: PROPOSE
my_files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py
your_files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py
note: My feature (get_image_mimetype lazy accessor on ImageBlock) touches the same file as your feature likely does. Since it's the same file, let's agree on non-overlapping regions: I will only add a new method get_image_mimetype() to ImageBlock and make minimal changes to image_to_base64 to call it (a few lines). Please tell me what method/region you need to touch so we avoid overlapping edits.

type: REVISE
my_files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py
your_files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py
note: We do overlap in the same file/method. Proposal for a clean split inside image_to_base64 (lines ~67-84): I will own the mimetype-guessing block (the 'if not self.image_mimetype: guess = filetype.guess...' lines) - extracting it into a new ImageBlock.get_image_mimetype() method that image_to_base64 calls. You own the image-bytes normalization/b64-encoding logic (your normalize_image_bytes helper + the 'self.image = base64.b64encode(self.image)' line). I will leave the b64-encoding line and your normalize_image_bytes untouched; please leave the mimetype-guessing logic to me. Go ahead and implement first if you want, or let me know your exact diff shape for image_to_base64 so I can rebase my one-line call to get_image_mimetype() around it. Reply ACCEPT if this split works for you.

type: REVISE
my_files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py
your_files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py
note: True disjoint split isn't possible since we both touch image_to_base64. Proposed boundary: I add a new module-level function normalize_image_bytes(image_bytes) -> (encoded_image, decoded_for_guessing) placed near the top of the file (before ImageBlock class), and change image_to_base64 to call it ONLY for the encode step (self.image = ...). You add your get_image_mimetype() method elsewhere in ImageBlock and wire it into image_to_base64 for the mimetype-guessing step only. We each submit independent patches, so a literal overlap in image_to_base64's body is fine as long as we don't fight over the same lines' intent. Suggested final shape: . Let me know if that works or if your get_image_mimetype() signature differs.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py
your_files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py
note: Agreed. I'll add ImageBlock.get_image_mimetype() (checks self.image_mimetype cache, else guesses via filetype.guess(self.image) and caches) and wire it into image_to_base64's mimetype-guessing step only. I won't touch the b64-encode line or any module-level normalize_image_bytes function you add. Proceeding to implement now.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py
your_files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py
note: Agreed. My exact plan for image_to_base64: keep the 'if not self.image: return self' guard untouched at top. Replace only the mimetype-guess block and the encode line with a call to normalize_image_bytes(self.image), which returns (encoded_image, decoded_for_guessing). New body: encoded_image, decoded_for_guessing = normalize_image_bytes(self.image) then 'if not self.image_mimetype:' block stays calling filetype.guess on decoded_for_guessing (or your get_image_mimetype(decoded_for_guessing) if you land first) then self.image = encoded_image at the end. I will NOT touch the mimetype-guessing conditional body itself - just the value it guesses on (decoded_for_guessing instead of self.image). If you land your get_image_mimetype() method first, I will call it with decoded_for_guessing as the arg. Proceeding to implement now.

type: DONE
my_files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py
your_files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py
note: Landed. Final get_image_mimetype() signature is no-arg: def get_image_mimetype(self) -> str | None, it reads/writes self.image_mimetype and guesses via filetype.guess(self.image) (i.e. it always guesses on self.image, not an arbitrary passed-in buffer). image_to_base64 now reads: 'if not self.image: return self' / 'self.get_image_mimetype()' / 'self.image = base64.b64encode(self.image)' / 'return self'. If your normalize_image_bytes needs to guess on a decoded/pre-encoded buffer different from self.image, please call filetype.guess directly in your own code path (or set self.image_mimetype yourself before calling self.image_to_base64/get_image_mimetype) rather than passing an arg into get_image_mimetype, since I'm keeping its signature argument-free per the PR spec. Tests pass (12 passed) on my end. Tell me if this breaks your plan and I'll adjust.

type: DONE
my_files: llama-index-core/llama_index/core/base/llms/types.py
note: Implemented normalize_image_bytes(image_bytes) -> (encoded_image, decoded_for_guessing) as a module-level function above TextBlock, and refactored image_to_base64 to: 1) call normalize_image_bytes(self.image) to get encoded_image/decoded_for_guessing, 2) guess mimetype via filetype.guess(decoded_for_guessing) if not already set, 3) assign self.image = encoded_image. I only changed the b64-encode/normalize path and the argument passed to filetype.guess (now decoded_for_guessing instead of self.image) - I left the guess/self.image_mimetype assignment lines as-is structurally so your get_image_mimetype() extraction should still slot in cleanly around that same block. Tests in tests/base/llms/test_types.py pass. Done on my end.
